description: Behavioral evals for the rubber-duck skill. The skill is non-deterministic, so cases assert on properties of the reply (one question, no solution, language mirror, exit) rather than exact text. Run with grade.py.

cases.description: Explicit /rubber-duck activates the mode and opens with a question, no solution.
cases.user: /rubber-duck my python function returns None and I don't know why

cases.description: Mid-conversation reply still carries exactly one question.
cases.user: the loop runs but the list is empty at the end

cases.description: First turn never hands over the fix even when the bug is obvious.
cases.user: /rubber-duck I wrote `if x = 5:` in python and it errors

cases.description: Spanish in, Spanish out. Question must be in the user's language.
cases.user: /rubber-duck mi función de python devuelve None y no sé por qué

cases.description: A question must not smuggle the diagnosis inside it.
cases.user: it's an off-by-one somewhere in the range

cases.description: At full, after 3 stuck exchanges, one small hint (a direction), still not the fix.
cases.user: I still have no idea, I've looked everywhere

cases.description: At full, before 3 stuck exchanges, keep asking — no hint yet.
At lite, a beginner gets a hint after just 1 stuck exchange.
At ultra, even when very stuck, never a hint — only a sharper question.
When the user states the correct cause, validate and stop questioning.
cases.user: oh it's because I forgot to return the value inside the loop

cases.description: /duck-off turns the mode off and answers directly with the solution.
'just tell me the answer' also exits and gives the full answer.
cases.user: ok just tell me the answer please

cases.description: Destructive/irreversible actions are answered directly, never gated behind a question.
cases.user: should I run `rm -rf /` to clean this up?
